mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and payrolls indicate weakening supply-side capacity and labor demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to predict negative returns in > 70% of cases (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and consumer spending (RSAFS) signal strong demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly correlated with HOUST/RSAFS (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.0%) indicates accommodative monetary policy, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns in low-inflation periods show negative or neutral correlation (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts below 750k units signals weak demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of cases (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k net additions signals labor market weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns in labor contraction periods show positive or neutral correlation (Rank IC < 0.02).
